articles.title: Rival Nations Seize On Choke Points to Counter Trump
articles.summary: From Iran to China, President Trump’s global aggression has encouraged other countries to search for new ways to pressure the U.S. economy.

articles.title: Radical Israeli settlers have stepped up attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli settlers have increased their attacks on Palestinians, while the military suspends a battalion that assaulted a CNN reporting team.

articles.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Trump Hails Destruction of Iranian Bridge, Warning ‘More to Follow’
articles.summary: Fars, a semiofficial news agency, said eight people had been killed in the attack and dozens wounded.

articles.title: Who Is Todd Blanche, Trump’s Acting Attorney General?
articles.summary: While it remains unclear how long Todd Blanche will stay in the job, whoever ends up taking over permanently will lead a department that he has shaped in his own image.

articles.title: Trump Says He’ll End the Iran War in Weeks. What If He Can’t?
articles.summary: President Trump faces the possibility that at the end of his own two-to-three week window for wrapping up the war in Iran, nothing much will have changed.

articles.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
articles.summary: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.

articles.title: Trump administration sues three states over attempts to regulate prediction markets
articles.summary: The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.

articles.title: In the Birthright Citizenship Hearing, a Story of Asians Fighting for Rights
articles.summary: In the Supreme Court’s oral arguments, lawyers and justices cited a litany of cases reflecting how long it took for Asians to win the right to be American.

articles.title: EPA flags microplastics, pharmaceuticals as contaminants in drinking water
articles.summary: There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.

articles.title: Trump’s Media-Bashing Is Coming Back to Bite Him in Court
articles.summary: Judges have cited attacks on the press by the president and his appointees when ruling against the government in at least three court cases.

articles.title: A President, the Supreme Court and a Landmark Citizenship Order Collide
articles.summary: The justices seems poised to rule against the president’s birthright citizenship plan. He is already furious over their decision rejecting his tariffs program.

articles.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi out at DOJ
articles.summary: Bondi's departure comes amid simmering frustration over her leadership and handling of the Epstein files. President Trump says Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche will be acting attorney general.

articles.title: In Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Case, Trump’s Likely Loss May Not End the Fight
articles.summary: The justices gave respectful consideration to what was once a fringe theory and could rule against it on grounds that would allow Congress to return to the question.

articles.title: Ziggy Stardust and Hacky Sack: What life was like the last time we went to the moon
articles.summary: The Artemis II mission is the first time humans have headed to the moon since 1972. That year also marked the debut of The Godfather and the Egg McMuffin.

articles.title: 'Stay Alive,' about daily life in Nazi Berlin, shows how easy it is to just go along
articles.summary: Historian Ian Buruma chronicles the lives of ordinary Berliners — including his own father — during World War II. Stay Alive is about the past, but has powerful lessons for the present.

articles.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
articles.summary: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

articles.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
articles.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

articles.title: As hoops betting spikes, it's New Hampshire and other states vs. prediction markets
articles.summary: In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.

articles.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
articles.summary: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

articles.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
articles.summary: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

articles.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
